Summary: WIN-EPIC-7: Windows Sandbox Backend
Description: Goal: replace preview classification and intentional blocking with real Windows enforcement.

Summary: WIN-701: Finalize Windows security model
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-7: Windows Sandbox Backend
Description: Finalize the Windows enforcement model and write down what functionally equivalent Windows security means for this product.
Acceptance Criteria:
- chosen Windows enforcement primitives are documented
- capability mapping is explicit for filesystem, network, process, IPC, and child lifecycle controls
- unsupported semantics are identified before implementation starts
- the meaning of functionally equivalent Windows security is written down

Summary: WIN-702: Implement process containment primitives
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-7: Windows Sandbox Backend
Description: Implement Windows child-process containment primitives as the first enforced runtime slice.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Windows child processes launch inside the chosen containment model
- parent/child lifetime rules are enforceable
- orphaned-process behavior is deterministic
- tests cover process lifecycle and containment failure paths

Summary: WIN-703: Implement filesystem restriction backend
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-7: Windows Sandbox Backend
Description: Implement the Windows filesystem restriction backend for supported allowlist behavior.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Windows enforces a supported subset of allowlist path restrictions
- denied paths fail deterministically
- protected paths remain blocked
- capability-to-policy mapping is documented and tested

Summary: WIN-704: Implement network enforcement backend
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-7: Windows Sandbox Backend
Description: Implement real Windows network enforcement for restricted live runs in the first full-port release.
Acceptance Criteria:
- live network restriction requests are truly enforced on Windows
- allow-all vs restricted network modes are distinct in runtime behavior
- blocked connections fail intentionally
- the implementation is documented and testable

Summary: WIN-705: Return deterministic unsupported results for non-mapped capabilities
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-7: Windows Sandbox Backend
Description: Ensure every unsupported or deferred capability returns an intentional result rather than advisory-only success.
Acceptance Criteria:
- each capability class reports supported, blocked, or deferred intentionally
- no silent advisory-only success remains in live restricted execution
- CLI messaging reflects backend reality

Summary: WIN-706: Wire enforced backend into live run path
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-7: Windows Sandbox Backend
Description: Connect the real Windows sandbox backend to the live Windows run path.
Acceptance Criteria:
- live restricted runs use the real Windows backend
- supported restrictions execute successfully
- unsupported restrictions fail clearly
- call paths are test-covered

Summary: WIN-EPIC-8: Supervisor Parity and Execution Hardening
Epic Name: WIN-EPIC-8: Supervisor Parity and Execution Hardening
Description: Goal: replace the current Windows supervisor scaffold with a real supervised runtime.

Summary: WIN-801: Implement Windows supervisor event loop
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-8: Windows Supervisor Parity and Execution Hardening
Description: Implement the parent event loop for supervised Windows execution.
Acceptance Criteria:
- the parent event loop runs on Windows
- startup and shutdown are stable
- supervisor diagnostics are actionable

Summary: WIN-802: Replace Unix fd-passing assumptions with Windows handle brokering
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-8: Windows Supervisor Parity and Execution Hardening
Description: Replace Unix fd-passing assumptions with Windows-safe handle brokering for supervised flows.
Acceptance Criteria:
- approved resources can be shared using Windows-safe handle transfer
- no Unix socket or SCM_RIGHTS dependency remains in supervised Windows flows
- transfer failure modes are testable

Summary: WIN-803: Implement runtime capability expansion on Windows
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-8: Windows Supervisor Parity and Execution Hardening
Description: Implement approved runtime capability expansion within the Windows supervisor model.
Acceptance Criteria:
- supervised requests can expand access within the Windows security model
- denied requests fail safely
- audit and diagnostics capture the decision path

Summary: WIN-804: Harden supervised teardown and recovery
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-8: Windows Supervisor Parity and Execution Hardening
Description: Harden cleanup, failure handling, and recovery semantics for supervised Windows sessions.
Acceptance Criteria:
- interrupted sessions clean up correctly
- failed supervisor startup does not leave ambiguous state
- rollback and audit hooks behave consistently on supervisor failure

Summary: WIN-805: Enable real supervised Windows run mode
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-8: Windows Supervisor Parity and Execution Hardening
Description: Promote supervised Windows mode from scaffold to real product path.
Acceptance Criteria:
- supervised Windows mode is no longer scaffold-only
- CLI output no longer reports preview limitation for active supervised features
- process-level Windows tests cover the real supervised path

Summary: WIN-EPIC-9: Filesystem, Rollback, State, and Keystore Parity
Epic Name: WIN-EPIC-9: Filesystem, Rollback, State, and Keystore Parity
Description: Goal: make Windows storage, path, rollback, and trust behavior production-usable.

Summary: WIN-901: Harden Windows path normalization and protected-path semantics
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-9: Filesystem, Rollback, State, and Keystore Parity
Description: Harden canonicalization and protected-path behavior for Windows-specific path semantics.
Acceptance Criteria:
- canonicalization is Windows-safe
- drive-letter, UNC, junction, and symlink behavior are handled intentionally
- protected-path checks remain fail-closed

Summary: WIN-902: Port rollback snapshot semantics to Windows
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-9: Filesystem, Rollback, State, and Keystore Parity
Description: Port rollback snapshot behavior to Windows workspaces.
Acceptance Criteria:
- rollback snapshots work on Windows workspaces
- exclusion rules and large-directory behavior are documented
- Windows file-lock behavior is handled intentionally

Summary: WIN-903: Validate state, audit, and runtime storage behavior on Windows
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-9: Filesystem, Rollback, State, and Keystore Parity
Description: Validate Windows state, audit, and runtime storage behavior for admin and user installs.
Acceptance Criteria:
- state roots resolve consistently for admin and user installs
- audit and runtime state do not depend on Unix conventions
- state migration or compatibility behavior is documented

Summary: WIN-904: Keystore parity for credential and trust flows
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-9: Filesystem, Rollback, State, and Keystore Parity
Description: Bring Windows keystore-backed credential and trust flows to production parity.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Windows keystore-backed flows work for supported trust and credential paths
- missing or inaccessible secret-store behavior fails clearly
- tests cover expected Windows secret-loading scenarios

Summary: WIN-905: Document Windows-specific filesystem and rollback semantics
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-9: Filesystem, Rollback, State, and Keystore Parity
Description: Document Windows-specific filesystem, path, and rollback behavior for operators and developers.
Acceptance Criteria:
- docs explain Windows-specific path and rollback behavior
- operator guidance is aligned with actual implementation limits

Summary: WIN-EPIC-10: CLI Feature Parity and Windows UX Completion
Epic Name: WIN-EPIC-10: CLI Feature Parity and Windows UX Completion
Description: Goal: remove preview-era product gaps for the main Windows CLI surface.

Summary: WIN-1001: Enable live profile-backed restricted runs on Windows
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-10: CLI Feature Parity and Windows UX Completion
Description: Enable live profile-backed restricted execution on Windows where backend support exists.
Acceptance Criteria:
- built-in profiles work in live mode where backend support exists
- unsupported profile controls fail intentionally
- output reflects real enforcement status

Summary: WIN-1002: Enable Windows shell mode or make a permanent product decision
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-10: CLI Feature Parity and Windows UX Completion
Description: Enable Windows shell mode or formalize a permanent product decision for it.
Acceptance Criteria:
- nono shell has a supported Windows behavior or a documented permanent limitation
- the chosen behavior is tested
- CLI help reflects the decision

Summary: WIN-1003: Enable wrap/apply semantics on Windows or make a permanent product decision
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-10: CLI Feature Parity and Windows UX Completion
Description: Enable wrap/apply semantics on Windows or formalize a permanent product decision for them.
Acceptance Criteria:
- nono wrap works correctly on Windows or is formally product-deferred
- no misleading enforcement claims remain

Summary: WIN-1004: Enable Windows trust interception and delegated open flows
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-10: CLI Feature Parity and Windows UX Completion
Description: Enable trust interception and delegated open flows in supported Windows supervised paths.
Acceptance Criteria:
- trust interception works in supported Windows supervised flows
- delegated browser or open flows are supported or intentionally limited
- diagnostics explain platform-specific behavior

Summary: WIN-1005: Promote help/setup/support output to first-class Windows messaging
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-10: CLI Feature Parity and Windows UX Completion
Description: Promote help, setup, and support output from preview-era wording to first-class Windows messaging.
Acceptance Criteria:
- preview-only language is removed where no longer true
- support, setup, and help text accurately reflect Windows support status
- docs and runtime output are aligned

Summary: WIN-EPIC-11: Windows Test Automation and Reliability
Epic Name: WIN-EPIC-11: Windows Test Automation and Reliability
Description: Goal: make Windows support regression-resistant and releaseable.

Summary: WIN-1101: Expand unit-test coverage for backend and supervisor flows
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-11: Windows Test Automation and Reliability
Description: Expand Windows unit coverage for backend and supervisor behaviors.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Windows backend and supervisor behaviors have targeted unit coverage
- security-sensitive failure paths are exercised by tests

Summary: WIN-1102: Build a Windows integration harness
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-11: Windows Test Automation and Reliability
Description: Build a Windows-native integration harness without Unix shell assumptions.
Acceptance Criteria:
- integration coverage runs on Windows without Unix shell assumptions
- key run, setup, profile, and rollback paths are covered
- test artifacts are useful for debugging failures

Summary: WIN-1103: Expand CI into enforced Windows test lanes
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-11: Windows Test Automation and Reliability
Description: Expand CI so Windows support is gated by enforced test lanes instead of ad hoc validation.
Acceptance Criteria:
- CI runs build, smoke, and integration subsets on Windows
- failures are isolated and debuggable
- Windows release confidence no longer depends on ad hoc local verification

Summary: WIN-1104: Add regression tests for Windows path and security edge cases
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-11: Windows Test Automation and Reliability
Description: Add Windows regression coverage for path and security edge cases.
Acceptance Criteria:
- path traversal, junction, symlink, UNC, and canonicalization edge cases are covered
- tests protect against silent security regressions

Summary: WIN-1105: Add Windows release-candidate validation checklist
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-11: Windows Test Automation and Reliability
Description: Add a formal Windows release-candidate validation checklist to the repo.
Acceptance Criteria:
- a formal Windows RC gate exists in-repo
- enforcement, packaging, and upgrade checks are part of release validation

Summary: WIN-EPIC-12: Packaging, Signing, and Release Readiness
Epic Name: WIN-EPIC-12: Packaging, Signing, and Release Readiness
Description: Goal: make Windows distribution supportable in real environments.

Summary: WIN-1201: Finalize Windows installer strategy
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-12: Packaging, Signing, and Release Readiness
Description: Finalize the supported Windows installer and artifact strategy for the full release.
Acceptance Criteria:
- MSI and zip delivery are the supported release paths, or a replacement decision is documented
- user vs machine install behavior is defined
- upgrade expectations are documented

Summary: WIN-1202: Add signed binary and installer workflow
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-12: Packaging, Signing, and Release Readiness
Description: Add signing support to the Windows binary and installer release workflow.
Acceptance Criteria:
- release automation supports signing
- binaries and installers are signed in the release path
- operator verification steps are documented

Summary: WIN-1203: Validate uninstall, upgrade, and repair behavior
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-12: Packaging, Signing, and Release Readiness
Description: Validate uninstall, upgrade, and repair behavior for Windows release artifacts.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Apps & Features uninstall works
- upgrade behavior is validated
- user and machine scope installs are both covered

Summary: WIN-1204: Add Windows service packaging if a service-capable runtime exists
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-12: Packaging, Signing, and Release Readiness
Description: Add Windows service packaging only if a real service-capable runtime exists.
Acceptance Criteria:
- this story only proceeds if a real Windows service runtime exists
- installer registration and service lifecycle work correctly
- uninstall and upgrade preserve service correctness

Summary: WIN-1205: Promote Windows to first-class release target
Epic Link: WIN-EPIC-12: Packaging, Signing, and Release Readiness
Description: Promote Windows from preview to first-class supported release target.
Acceptance Criteria:
- docs, release automation, and support messaging all reflect first-class Windows support
- Windows is no longer described as preview
